contentBlocks.original:
आनन्दमन्थरपुरन्दरमुक्तमाल्यं
मौलौ हठेन निहितं महिषासुरस्य ।
पादाम्बुजं भवतु मे विजयाय मञ्जु-
-मञ्जीरशिञ्जितमनोहरमम्बिकायाः ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānandamantharapurandaramuktamālyaṃ
maulau haṭhēna nihitaṃ mahiṣāsurasya ।
pādāmbujaṃ bhavatu mē vijayāya mañju-
-mañjīraśiñjitamanōharamambikāyāḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the lotus feet of Ambikā — charming and lovely with their tinkling anklets, forcibly placed on the head of Mahiṣāsura (who was in the throes of the joy of Indra's released garland) — prevail for my victory.
contentBlocks.meanings: ānandam anthara-purandara-mukta-mālyaṃ — Indra's released garland being in the midst of joy; maulau haṭhena nihitaṃ mahiṣāsurasya — forcibly placed on the head of Mahiṣāsura; pādāmbujam bhavatu me vijayāya — may the lotus feet grant me victory; mañju-mañjīra-śiñjita-manoharam ambikāyāḥ — the charming and lovely anklet-jingling (feet) of Ambikā.

contentBlocks.original:
देवि त्र्यम्बकपत्नि पार्वति सति त्रैलोक्यमातः शिवे
शर्वाणि त्रिपुरे मृडानि वरदे रुद्राणि कात्यायनि ।
भीमे भैरवि चण्डि शर्वरिकले कालक्षये शूलिनि
त्वत्पादप्रणताननन्यमनसः पर्याकुलान्पाहि नः ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvi tryambakapatni pārvati sati trailōkyamātaḥ śivē
śarvāṇi tripurē mṛḍāni varadē rudrāṇi kātyāyani ।
bhīmē bhairavi chaṇḍi śarvarikalē kālakṣayē śūlini
tvatpādapraṇatānananyamanasaḥ paryākulānpāhi naḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, O wife of Tryambaka, Pārvatī, Satī, Mother of the three worlds, Śivā, Śarvaṇī, Tripurā, Mṛḍānī, Varadā, Rudrāṇī, Kātyāyanī, Bhīmā, Bhairavī, Caṇḍī, Śarvarī, Kalā, Śūlinī at the end of time — protect us who, bowing to your feet with undivided minds, are deeply troubled.
contentBlocks.meanings: devi — O Goddess; tryambaka-patni — wife of Tryambaka (Śiva); pārvati — Pārvatī; sati — Satī; trailokya-māta — Mother of the three worlds; śive — O auspicious one; śarvaṇi — Śarvaṇī; tripure — Tripurā; mṛḍāni — Mṛḍānī; varade — Varadā (boon-giver); rudrāṇi — Rudrāṇī; kātyāyani — Kātyāyanī; bhīme — O fearsome one; bhairavi — Bhairavī; caṇḍi — Caṇḍī; śarvari-kale — Śarvarī (night) / Kalā; kāla-kṣaye śūlini — Śūlinī at time's end; tvat-pāda-praṇatān ananya-manasaḥ — us who, bowing to your feet with undivided minds; paryākulān pāhi naḥ — protect us who are greatly troubled.

contentBlocks.original:
देवि त्वां सकृदेव यः प्रणमति क्षोणीभृतस्तं नम-
-न्त्याजन्मस्फुरदङ्घ्रिपीठविलुठत्कोटीरकोटिच्छटाः ।
यस्त्वामर्चति सोऽर्च्यते सुरगणैर्यः स्तौति स स्तूयते
यस्त्वां ध्यायति तं स्मरार्तिविधुरा ध्यायन्ति वामभ्रुवः ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvi tvāṃ sakṛdēva yaḥ praṇamati kṣōṇībhṛtastaṃ nama-
-ntyājanmasphuradaṅghripīṭhaviluṭhatkōṭīrakōṭichChaṭāḥ ।
yastvāmarchati sō'rchyatē suragaṇairyaḥ stauti sa stūyatē
yastvāṃ dhyāyati taṃ smarārtividhurā dhyāyanti vāmabhruvaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, the one who prostrates to you even once — kings bow down to him, their crowns glittering at the footstool he sits upon, from birth onward. The one who worships you is worshipped by the hosts of gods; the one who praises you is praised; the one who meditates on you — lovely-browed women, tormented by Love's affliction, meditate on him.
contentBlocks.meanings: devi tvāṃ sakṛt eva yaḥ praṇamati — O Goddess, the one who prostrates to you even once; kṣoṇī-bhṛtaḥ taṃ namanti — kings bow to him; ā-janma-sphurad-aṅghri-pīṭha-viluṭhat-koṭīra-koṭi-cchaTāḥ — their crowns glittering at the footstool from birth onward; yas tvāṃ arcati so'rcyate sura-gaṇaiḥ — the one who worships you is worshipped by hosts of gods; yaḥ stauti sa stūyate — the one who praises you is praised; yas tvāṃ dhyāyati taṃ smarārti-vidhurā dhyāyanti vāma-bhruvaḥ — the one who meditates on you — lovely-browed women, tormented by the pain of Love, meditate on him.

contentBlocks.original:
उन्मत्ता इव सग्रहा इव विषव्यासक्तमूर्छा इव
प्राप्तप्रौढमदा इवार्तिविरहग्रस्ता इवार्ता इव ।
ये ध्यायन्ति हि शैलराजतनयां धन्यास्त एवाग्रतः
त्यक्तोपाधिविवृद्धरागमनसो ध्यायन्ति तान्सुभ्रुवः ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
unmattā iva sagrahā iva viṣavyāsaktamūrChā iva
prāptaprauḍhamadā ivārtivirahagrastā ivārtā iva ।
yē dhyāyanti hi śailarājatanayāṃ dhanyāsta ēvāgrataḥ
tyaktōpādhivivṛddharāgamanasō dhyāyanti tānsubhruvaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Like those in frenzy, like those seized by planets, like those stupefied by poison, like those who have attained full intoxication, like those tormented by separation's anguish — those who meditate on the daughter of the king of mountains: they alone are the blessed ones. Those who have abandoned limiting conditions and whose minds are filled with pure Love meditate on them, (the lovely-browed ones).
contentBlocks.meanings: unmattā iva — like those in frenzy; sa-grahā iva — like those seized by planetary spirits; viṣa-vyāsakta-mūrchhā iva — like those stupefied by poison; prāpta-prauḍha-madā iva — like those who have attained full intoxication; ārti-viraha-grastā iva ārtā iva — like those tormented by the anguish of separation; ye dhyāyanti hi śaila-rāja-tanayāṃ — those who meditate on the daughter of the mountain king; dhanyāḥ te eva agrataḥ — they alone are the blessed ones foremost; tyakta-upādhi-vivṛddha-rāga-manasaḥ — whose minds have abandoned limiting conditions and are filled with pure Love; dhyāyanti tān subhruvaḥ — lovely-browed (women) meditate on them.

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यायन्ति ये क्षणमपि त्रिपुरे हृदि त्वां
लावण्ययौवनधनैरपि विप्रयुक्ताः ।
ते विस्फुरन्ति ललितायतलोचनानां
चित्तैकभित्तिलिखितप्रतिमाः पुमांसः ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāyanti yē kṣaṇamapi tripurē hṛdi tvāṃ
lāvaṇyayauvanadhanairapi viprayuktāḥ ।
tē visphuranti lalitāyatalōchanānāṃ
chittaikabhittilikhitapratimāḥ pumāṃsaḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who meditate on you, O Tripurā, in the heart even for a moment — though separated from the wealth of beauty and youth — they shine forth in the minds of large-eyed women as images painted on the sole wall of their hearts.
contentBlocks.meanings: dhyāyanti ye kṣaṇam api tripure hṛdi tvāṃ — those who meditate on you, O Tripurā, in the heart even for a moment; lāvaṇya-yauvana-dhanaiḥ api viprayuktāḥ — though separated from the wealth of beauty and youth; te visphuranati — they shine forth; lalita-āyata-locanānāṃ — in (the minds of) women with large and lovely eyes; citta-eka-bhitti-likhita-pratimāḥ pumāṃsaḥ — men (painted) as images on the sole wall of their hearts.

contentBlocks.original:
एतं किं नु दृशा पिबाम्युत विशाम्यस्याङ्गमङ्गैर्निजैः
किं वाऽमुं निगराम्यनेन सहसा किं वैकतामाश्रये ।
यस्येत्थं विवशो विकल्पललिताकूतेन योषिज्जनः
किं तद्यन्न करोति देवि हृदये यस्य त्वमावर्तसे ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētaṃ kiṃ nu dṛśā pibāmyuta viśāmyasyāṅgamaṅgairnijaiḥ
kiṃ vā'muṃ nigarāmyanēna sahasā kiṃ vaikatāmāśrayē ।
yasyētthaṃ vivaśō vikalpalalitākūtēna yōṣijjanaḥ
kiṃ tadyanna karōti dēvi hṛdayē yasya tvamāvartasē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shall I drink this one with my eyes? Or shall I enter her body with my own limbs? Or shall I swallow her suddenly? Or shall I become one with her? Whatever strange, confused fancies captivated by the beautiful Lalitā — a woman's mind makes a man do in this way: what is there that it does not do, O Goddess, in the heart of one in whom you dwell?
contentBlocks.meanings: etaṃ kiṃ nu dṛśā pibāmi — shall I drink this one with my eyes?; uta viśāmi asya aṅgam aṅgaiḥ nijaiḥ — or shall I enter her body with my own limbs?; kiṃ vā amum nigarāmi anena sahasā — or shall I swallow her suddenly?; kiṃ vā ekatām āśraye — or shall I become one with her?; yasya itthaṃ vivaśaḥ vikalpa-lalitā-kūtena yoṣij-janaḥ — of one made helpless by the caprice of the beautiful Lalitā's fancies, a woman; kiṃ tat yan na karoti — what is there that she does not make him do?; devi hṛdaye yasya tvam āvartase — O Goddess, in the heart of one in whom you dwell.

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वव्यापिनि यद्वदीश्वर इति स्थाणावनन्याश्रयः
शब्दः शक्तिरिति त्रिलोकजननि त्वय्येव तथ्यस्थितिः ।
इत्थं सत्यपि शक्नुवन्ति यदिमाः क्षुद्रा रुजो बाधितुं
त्वद्भक्तानपि न क्षिणोषि च रुषा तद्देवि चित्रं महत् ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvavyāpini yadvadīśvara iti sthāṇāvananyāśrayaḥ
śabdaḥ śaktiriti trilōkajanani tvayyēva tathyasthitiḥ ।
itthaṃ satyapi śaknuvanti yadimāḥ kṣudrā rujō bādhituṃ
tvadbhaktānapi na kṣiṇōṣi cha ruṣā taddēvi chitraṃ mahat ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as the word 'Īśvara' has exclusive, invariable application to the Immovable One (Śiva), so the term 'Śakti' truly applies to you alone, O Mother of the three worlds. Yet, given that this is so, how do these petty afflictions manage to trouble even your devotees? And you too do not wipe them out in anger — O Goddess, this is a great wonder.
contentBlocks.meanings: viśva-vyāpini — O all-pervading one; yadvat īśvara iti sthāṇau ananya-āśrayaḥ śabdaḥ — just as the word 'Īśvara' has exclusive application to the Immovable (Śiva); śaktiḥ iti tri-loka-janani tvayyeva tathya-sthitiḥ — so the word 'Śakti' truly applies to you alone, O Mother of the three worlds; itthaṃ sati api śaknuvanti yad imāḥ kṣudrā rujaḥ bādhituṃ tvad-bhaktān api — yet, given this, these petty afflictions are able to trouble even your devotees; na kṣiṇoṣi ca ruṣā — and you too do not wipe them out in anger; tat devi citram mahat — O Goddess, this is a great wonder.

contentBlocks.original:
इन्दोर्मध्यगतां मृगाङ्कसदृशच्छायां मनोहारिणीं
पाण्डूत्फुल्लसरोरुहासनगता स्निग्धप्रदीपच्छविम् ।
वर्षन्तीममृतं भवानि भवतीं ध्यायन्ति ये देहिनः
ते निर्मुक्तरुजो भवन्ति रिपवः प्रोज्झन्ति तान्दूरतः ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indōrmadhyagatāṃ mṛgāṅkasadṛśachChāyāṃ manōhāriṇīṃ
pāṇḍūtphullasarōruhāsanagatā snigdhapradīpachChavim ।
varṣantīmamṛtaṃ bhavāni bhavatīṃ dhyāyanti yē dēhinaḥ
tē nirmuktarujō bhavanti ripavaḥ prōjjhanti tāndūrataḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who meditate on you — O Bhavānī, seated on the white fully-bloomed lotus throne, stationed within the moon's disc, of moonlike lovely shadow, with the beauty of a tender lamp-flame, showering nectar — they become free of disease, and their enemies abandon them from afar.
contentBlocks.meanings: indoḥ madhya-gatāṃ — stationed within the moon's disc; mṛgāṅka-sadṛśa-cchāyāṃ manoháriṇīṃ — of moonlike lovely shadow; pāṇḍu-utphulla-saroruha-āsana-gatā — seated on the white fully-bloomed lotus throne; snigdha-pradīpa-cchavim — with the beauty of a tender lamp-flame; varṣantīm amṛtaṃ bhavāni bhavatīṃ dhyāyanti ye dehinaḥ — those embodied beings who meditate on you, O Bhavānī, showering nectar; te nirmukta-rujaḥ bhavanti — they become free of disease; ripavaḥ projjhanti tān dūrataḥ — and their enemies abandon them from afar.

contentBlocks.original:
पूर्णेन्दोः शकलैरिवातिबहलैः पीयूषपूरैरिव
क्षीराब्धेर्लहरीभरैरिव सुधापङ्कस्य पिण्डैरिव ।
प्रालेयैरिव निर्मितं तव वपुर्ध्यायन्ति ये श्रद्धया
चित्तान्तर्निहितार्तितापविपदस्ते सम्पदं बिभ्रति ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pūrṇēndōḥ śakalairivātibahalaiḥ pīyūṣapūrairiva
kṣīrābdhērlaharībharairiva sudhāpaṅkasya piṇḍairiva ।
prālēyairiva nirmitaṃ tava vapurdhyāyanti yē śraddhayā
chittāntarnihitārtitāpavipadastē sampadaṃ bibhrati ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who, with faith, meditate on your body — as if fashioned of the thick slices of the full moon, as if of flood-streams of nectar, as if of masses of waves from the milk-ocean, as if of lumps of nectar-mud, as if of snowflakes — they bear within them the wealth of prosperity, having dispelled the afflictions, suffering, and calamities hidden in their hearts.
contentBlocks.meanings: pūrṇendoḥ śakalaiḥ iva atibahulaiḥ — as if of very thick slices of the full moon; pīyūṣa-pūraiḥ iva — as if of flood-streams of nectar; kṣīra-abdheḥ laharī-bharaiḥ iva — as if of masses of waves from the milk-ocean; sudhā-paṅkasya piṇḍaiḥ iva — as if of lumps of nectar-mud; prāleyaiḥ iva nirmitaṃ tava vapuḥ dhyāyanti ye śraddhayā — those who meditate with faith on your body as if fashioned of snowflakes; citta-antaḥ-nihita-ārti-tāpa-vipadaḥ te sampadaṃ bibhrati — those (ones), having dispelled afflictions, suffering, and calamities hidden in the heart, bear prosperity.

contentBlocks.original:
ये संस्मरन्ति तरलां सहसोल्लसन्तीं
त्वां ग्रन्थिपञ्चकभिदं तरुणार्कशोणाम् ।
रागार्णवे बहलरागिणि मज्जयन्तीं
कृत्स्नं जगद्दधति चेतसि तान्मृगाक्ष्यः ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yē saṃsmaranti taralāṃ sahasōllasantīṃ
tvāṃ granthipañchakabhidaṃ taruṇārkaśōṇām ।
rāgārṇavē bahalarāgiṇi majjayantīṃ
kṛtsnaṃ jagaddadhati chētasi tānmṛgākṣyaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who hold in mind — of a rosy hue like the young sun, suddenly flashing forth, piercing the five knots, deep red in deep passion, drowning the whole world in the ocean of passion — that restless you: deer-eyed women bear them in their hearts.
contentBlocks.meanings: ye saṃsmaranti taralāṃ sahasā ullasantīṃ — those who hold in mind the restless (you) suddenly flashing forth; tvāṃ granthi-pañcaka-bhidaṃ — (you) who pierce the five knots (of the five chakras); taruṇa-arka-śoṇāṃ — rosy as the young sun; rāga-arṇave bahala-rāgiṇi majjayantīṃ — drowning in the ocean of passion that which is deeply passionate; kṛtsnaṃ jagat — the whole world; dadhati cetasi tān mṛgākṣyaḥ — deer-eyed women bear those men in their hearts.

contentBlocks.original:
लाक्षारसस्नपितपङ्कजतन्तुतन्वीं
अन्तः स्मरत्यनुदिनं भवतीं भवानि ।
यस्तं स्मरप्रतिममप्रतिमस्वरूपाः
नेत्रोत्पलैर्मृगदृशो भृशमर्चयन्ति ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lākṣārasasnapitapaṅkajatantutanvīṃ
antaḥ smaratyanudinaṃ bhavatīṃ bhavāni ।
yastaṃ smarapratimamapratimasvarūpāḥ
nētrōtpalairmṛgadṛśō bhṛśamarchayanti ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The one who meditates daily within on you, O Bhavānī — slender as a lotus filament soaked in lac-juice — him, women whose forms rival Kāma himself, of incomparable beauty, ardently worship with the lotuses of their eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: lākṣā-rasa-snapita-paṅkaja-tantu-tanvīṃ — slender as a lotus filament soaked in lac-juice; antaḥ smarati anudinaṃ bhavatīṃ bhavāni — who meditates within on you daily, O Bhavānī; yas taṃ — that one; smara-pratimam apratima-svarūpāḥ — women whose forms rival Kāma himself, of incomparable beauty; netra-utpalaiḥ mṛga-dṛśaḥ bhṛśam arcayanti — deer-eyed women ardently worship with the lotuses of their eyes.

contentBlocks.original:
स्तुमस्त्वां वाचमव्यक्तां हिमकुन्देन्दुरोचिषम् ।
कदम्बमालां बिभ्राणामापादतललम्बिनीम् ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stumastvāṃ vāchamavyaktāṃ himakundēndurōchiṣam ।
kadambamālāṃ bibhrāṇāmāpādatalalambinīm ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: We praise you — the unmanifest Speech, white as frost, jasmine, and moon, bearing a garland of kadamba flowers hanging down to the feet.
contentBlocks.meanings: stummas tvāṃ vācam avyaktāṃ — we praise you, the unmanifest Speech; hima-kunda-indu-rociṣam — white as frost, jasmine, and moon; kadamba-mālāṃ bibhrāṇām — bearing a garland of kadamba flowers; āpāda-tala-lambinīm — hanging down to the feet.

contentBlocks.original:
मूर्ध्नीन्दोः सितपङ्कजासनगतां प्रालेयपाण्डुत्विषं
वर्षन्तीममृतं सरोरुहभुवो वक्त्रेऽपि रन्ध्रेऽपि च ।
अच्छिन्ना च मनोहरा च ललिता चातिप्रसन्नापि च
त्वामेवं स्मरतः स्मरारिदयिते वाक्सर्वतो वल्गति ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūrdhnīndōḥ sitapaṅkajāsanagatāṃ prālēyapāṇḍutviṣaṃ
varṣantīmamṛtaṃ sarōruhabhuvō vaktrē'pi randhrē'pi cha ।
achChinnā cha manōharā cha lalitā chātiprasannāpi cha
tvāmēvaṃ smarataḥ smarāridayitē vāksarvatō valgati ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of one who thus meditates on you — stationed at the crown within the moon, of snow-white radiance, showering nectar upon the face too and upon the brahmarandhra, uninterrupted and beautiful and graceful and exceedingly serene — O beloved of the enemy of Love, speech flows everywhere.
contentBlocks.meanings: mūrdhni indoḥ sita-paṅkaja-āsana-gatāṃ — stationed at the crown within the moon, seated on a white lotus; prāleya-pāṇḍu-tviṣaṃ — of snow-white radiance; varṣantīm amṛtaṃ — showering nectar; saroruha-bhuvaḥ vaktre api randhre api ca — upon the face too and upon the brahmarandhra (crown opening); acchinnā ca manohārā ca lalitā ca atiprasannā api ca — uninterrupted and beautiful and graceful and exceedingly serene; tvām evaṃ smarataḥ smara-āri-dayite — of one who meditates on you thus, O beloved of the enemy of Love (Śiva); vāk sarvataḥ valgati — speech flows everywhere.

contentBlocks.original:
ददातीष्टान्भोगान् क्षपयति रिपून्हन्ति विपदो
दहत्याधीन्व्याधीन् शमयति सुखानि प्रतनुते ।
हठादन्तर्दुःखं दलयति पिनष्टीष्टविरहं
सकृद्ध्याता देवी किमिव निरवद्यं न कुरुते ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dadātīṣṭānbhōgān kṣapayati ripūnhanti vipadō
dahatyādhīnvyādhīn śamayati sukhāni pratanutē ।
haṭhādantarduḥkhaṃ dalayati pinaṣṭīṣṭavirahaṃ
sakṛddhyātā dēvī kimiva niravadyaṃ na kurutē ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She grants desired enjoyments, destroys enemies, kills calamities, burns mental anguish and physical disease, bestows pleasures, with force splits inner suffering, grinds the pain of separation from the beloved — what does the Goddess, meditated upon even once, not accomplish blamelessly?
contentBlocks.meanings: dadāti iṣṭān bhogān — she grants desired enjoyments; kṣapayati ripūn — destroys enemies; hanti vipadaḥ — kills calamities; dahati ādhīn vyādhīn — burns mental anguish and physical disease; śamayati sukhāni pratanute — bestows pleasures; haṭhāt antaḥ-duḥkhaṃ dalayati — with force splits inner suffering; pinaṣṭi iṣṭa-virahaṃ — grinds the pain of separation from the beloved; sakṛt dhyātā devī kiṃ iva niravadyaṃ na kurute — what does the Goddess, meditated upon even once, not accomplish blamelessly?

contentBlocks.original:
यस्त्वां ध्यायति वेत्ति विन्दति जपत्यालोकते चिन्तय-
-त्यन्वेति प्रतिपद्यते कलयति स्तौत्याश्रयत्यर्चति ।
यश्च त्र्यम्बकवल्लभे तव गुणानाकर्णयत्यादरात्
तस्य श्रीर्न गृहादपैति विजयस्तस्याग्रतो धावति ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yastvāṃ dhyāyati vētti vindati japatyālōkatē chintaya-
-tyanvēti pratipadyatē kalayati stautyāśrayatyarchati ।
yaścha tryambakavallabhē tava guṇānākarṇayatyādarāt
tasya śrīrna gṛhādapaiti vijayastasyāgratō dhāvati ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The one who meditates on you, knows you, attains you, recites your name, visualizes you, contemplates you, follows you, apprehends you, honours you, praises you — and also the one who respectfully listens to your qualities, O beloved of Tryambaka — Lakṣmī never departs from his home, and victory runs before him.
contentBlocks.meanings: yaḥ tvāṃ dhyāyati — who meditates on you; vetti — knows; vindati — attains; japati — recites; ālokate — visualizes; cintayati — contemplates; anveti — follows; pratipadyate — apprehends; kalayati — honours; stauti — praises; āśrayati — takes refuge in; arcati — worships; yaḥ ca tryambaka-vallabhe tava guṇān ākarṇayati ādarāt — and the one who respectfully listens to your qualities, O beloved of Tryambaka; tasya śrīḥ na gṛhāt apaiti — Lakṣmī never departs from his home; vijayaḥ tasya agrataḥ dhāvati — victory runs before him.

contentBlocks.original:
किं किं दुःखं दनुजदलिनि क्षीयते न स्मृतायां
का का कीर्तिः कुलकमलिनि ख्याप्यते न स्तुतायाम् ।
का का सिद्धिः सुरवरनुते प्राप्यते नार्चितायां
कं कं योगं त्वयि न चिनुते चित्तमालम्बितायाम् ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kiṃ kiṃ duḥkhaṃ danujadalini kṣīyatē na smṛtāyāṃ
kā kā kīrtiḥ kulakamalini khyāpyatē na stutāyām ।
kā kā siddhiḥ suravaranutē prāpyatē nārchitāyāṃ
kaṃ kaṃ yōgaṃ tvayi na chinutē chittamālambitāyām ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O destroyer of demons, what suffering is not dissolved when you are remembered? O garland of the lotus clan, what glory is not proclaimed when you are praised? O honoured by the best of gods, what siddhi is not attained when you are worshipped? What yoga does the mind not accumulate by depending on you?
contentBlocks.meanings: kiṃ kiṃ duḥkhaṃ danuja-dalani kṣīyate na smṛtāyāṃ — O destroyer of demons, what suffering is not dissolved when you are remembered?; kā kā kīrtiḥ kula-kamalini khyāpyate na stutāyāṃ — O garland of the lotus clan, what glory is not proclaimed when you are praised?; kā kā siddhiḥ sura-vara-nute prāpyate na arcitāyāṃ — O honoured by the best of gods, what siddhi is not attained when you are worshipped?; kaṃ kaṃ yogaṃ tvayi na cinute cittam ālambhitāyāṃ — what yoga does the mind not accumulate by depending on you?

contentBlocks.original:
ये देवि दुर्धरकृतान्तमुखान्तरस्थाः
ये कालि कालघनपाशनितान्तबद्धाः ।
ये चण्डि चण्डगुरुकल्मषसिन्धुमग्नाः
तान्पासि मोचयसि तारयसि स्मृतैव ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yē dēvi durdharakṛtāntamukhāntarasthāḥ
yē kāli kālaghanapāśanitāntabaddhāḥ ।
yē chaṇḍi chaṇḍagurukalmaṣasindhumagnāḥ
tānpāsi mōchayasi tārayasi smṛtaiva ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Devī, those who stand in the jaws of the hard-to-resist God of Death — O Kālī, those firmly bound in the noose of the dense clouds of time — O Caṇḍī, those submerged in the ocean of grave and heavy sin — you protect them, release them, and ferry them across, merely by being remembered.
contentBlocks.meanings: ye devi durdhara-kṛtānta-mukha-antasthaḥ — O Goddess, those who stand within the jaws of the hard-to-resist God of Death; ye kāli kāla-ghana-pāśa-nitānta-baddhāḥ — O Kālī, those firmly bound in the noose of dense clouds of time; ye caṇḍi caṇḍa-guru-kalmaṣa-sindhu-magnāḥ — O Caṇḍī, those submerged in the ocean of fierce and heavy sin; tān pāsi mocayasi tārayasi smṛtā eva — you protect, release, and ferry them across, merely by being remembered.

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीवशीकरणचूर्णसहोदराणि
त्वत्पादपङ्कजरजांसि चिरं जयन्ति ।
यानि प्रणाममिलितानि नृणां ललाटे
लुम्पन्ति दैवलिखितानि दुरक्षराणि ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīvaśīkaraṇachūrṇasahōdarāṇi
tvatpādapaṅkajarajāṃsi chiraṃ jayanti ।
yāni praṇāmamilitāni nṛṇāṃ lalāṭē
lumpanti daivalikhitāni durakṣarāṇi ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The dust of your lotus feet long prevails — sisters of the powder that captivates Lakṣmī — which, gathered at the forehead in the act of prostration, erases the ill-written letters inscribed by fate.
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmī-vaśīkaraṇa-cūrṇa-sahodarāṇi — sisters of the powder that captivates Lakṣmī; tvat-pāda-paṅkaja-rajāṃsi ciraṃ jayanti — the dust of your lotus feet long prevails; yāni praṇāma-militāni nṛṇāṃ lalāṭe — which, gathering at the forehead in the act of prostration; lumpanti daiva-likhitāni dur-akṣarāṇi — erase the ill-written letters inscribed by fate.

contentBlocks.original:
रे मूढाः किमयं वृथैव तपसा कायः परिक्लिश्यते
यज्ञैर्वा बहुदक्षिणैः किमितरे रिक्तीक्रियन्ते गृहाः ।
भक्तिश्चेदविनाशिनी भगवतीपादद्वयी सेव्यतां
उन्निद्राम्बुरुहातपत्रसुभगा लक्ष्मीः पुरो धावति ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rē mūḍhāḥ kimayaṃ vṛthaiva tapasā kāyaḥ parikliśyatē
yajñairvā bahudakṣiṇaiḥ kimitarē riktīkriyantē gṛhāḥ ।
bhaktiśchēdavināśinī bhagavatīpādadvayī sēvyatāṃ
unnidrāmburuhātapatrasubhagā lakṣmīḥ purō dhāvati ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You fools! Why is the body unnecessarily tormented by austerities? Why are others' homes emptied through expensive sacrifices with great dakṣiṇā? If undying devotion exists, let the twin feet of the Goddess be served — then Lakṣmī, beautiful as an open lotus parasol, runs forward.
contentBlocks.meanings: re mūḍhāḥ — you fools!; kim ayaṃ vṛthā eva tapasā kāyaḥ parikliśyate — why is the body unnecessarily tormented by austerities?; yajñaiḥ vā bahu-dakṣiṇaiḥ kim itare riktī-kriyante gṛhāḥ — or why are others' homes emptied through sacrifices with great dakṣiṇā fees?; bhaktiḥ cet avināśinī bhagavatī-pāda-dvayī sevyatāṃ — if undying devotion exists, let the twin feet of the Goddess be served; un-nidra-ambu-ruha-ātapatra-subhagā lakṣmīḥ puraḥ dhāvati — then Lakṣmī, beautiful as a parasol of open lotuses, runs forward.

contentBlocks.original:
याचे न कञ्चन न कञ्चन वञ्चयामि
सेवे न कञ्चन निरस्तसमस्तदैन्यः ।
श्लक्ष्णं वसे मधुरमद्मि भजे वरस्त्रीः
देवी हृदि स्फुरति मे कुलकामधेनुः ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāchē na kañchana na kañchana vañchayāmi
sēvē na kañchana nirastasamastadainyaḥ ।
ślakṣṇaṃ vasē madhuramadmi bhajē varastrīḥ
dēvī hṛdi sphurati mē kulakāmadhēnuḥ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I beg of no one, I deceive no one, having abandoned all wretchedness. I serve no one. I dress comfortably, eat sweetly, enjoy beautiful women — because the Goddess, the wish-fulfilling cow of my clan, shines in my heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: yāce na kañcana — I beg of no one; na kañcana vañcayāmi — I deceive no one; seve na kañcana — I serve no one; nirasta-samasta-dainyaḥ — having abandoned all wretchedness; ślakṣṇaṃ vase — I dress comfortably; madhuram admi — I eat sweetly; bhaje vara-strīḥ — I enjoy beautiful women; devī hṛdi sphurati me kula-kāma-dhenuḥ — because the Goddess, the wish-fulfilling cow of my clan, shines in my heart.

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि यामिनीनाथलेखालङ्कृतकुन्तलाम् ।
भवानीं भवसन्तापनिर्वापणसुधानदीम् ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi yāminīnāthalēkhālaṅkṛtakuntalām ।
bhavānīṃ bhavasantāpanirvāpaṇasudhānadīm ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to her whose locks are adorned with the crescent moon — to Bhavānī, the river of nectar that extinguishes the fever of existence.
contentBlocks.meanings: namāmi yāminī-nātha-lekhā-alaṅkṛta-kuntalāṃ — I bow to her whose locks are adorned with the crescent of the moon (lord of night); bhavānīṃ — Bhavānī; bhava-santāpa-nirvāpaṇa-sudhā-nadīm — the river of nectar that extinguishes the burning of existence.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीकालिदास विरचित पञ्चस्तव्यां तृतीयः घटस्तवः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīkāḻidāsa virachita pañchastavyāṃ tṛtīyaḥ ghaṭastavaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Here ends the Ghaṭa Stava, the third of the Pañchastavya composed by the illustrious Kālidāsa.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti — here ends; śrī-kālidāsa-viracita — composed by the illustrious Kālidāsa; pañcastavyāṃ — of the Pañchastavya (Five Hymns); tṛtīyaḥ — the third; ghaṭa-stavaḥ — the Ghaṭa Stava.
